title: The first line of every Precept definition: `precept Entity`. This is the moment when the contract comes into existence. The `precept` keyword is in VS Code blue; the entity name is in amber; the blinking cursor is the bright accent mark that says 'this definition is actively being authored.' The icon captures the one instant before the compiler compiles — the authorship moment that begins the governing contract.
conceptualBridge: The `precept Entity|` cursor means Precept's definition IS the product because every governed entity begins with this exact line — the icon is not the tool's UI but the act of creating a governing contract.
decision: Rejected: legibility hard fail — `precept Entity` text at 96px font renders as ~6px at 32px scale; completely unreadable. The concept of authorship/declaration is worth revisiting with a 1-2 character symbol rather than multi-word text.
